title: × laeliocattleya welsiana

side_effects: × laeliocattleya welsiana can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, or respiratory issues due to the plant's sap or pollen. Ingesting any part of the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while prolonged exposure to its pollen may trigger asthma attacks in sensitive individuals. Furthermore, the plant's sap may cause blisters or rashes on the skin, particularly if it comes into contact with open wounds.
